content.text: | MCP:meqhpA | successfully downloaded text file (SHA: d202c38a9dd8172e76072e244ad5292d61b67435)

| MCP:meqhpA | SHELL := bash
| MCP:meqhpA | .ONESHELL:
| MCP:meqhpA | .SHELLFLAGS := -eu -o pipefail -c
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | export DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1
| MCP:meqhpA | export COMPOSE_DOCKER_CLI_BUILD=1
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | targets: help
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | up: ## Run the application
| MCP:meqhpA | 	docker compose up -d --build api
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | done: check test ## Prepare for a commit
| MCP:meqhpA | test: utest itest  ## Run unit and integration tests
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | ci-compose := docker compose -f .ci/docker-compose.yml
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | utest: cleantest ## Run unit tests
| MCP:meqhpA | 	$(ci-compose) run --rm unit pytest -m unit .
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | itest: cleantest ## Run integration tests
| MCP:meqhpA | 	$(ci-compose) run --rm integration pytest -m integration .
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | check: cleantest ## Check the code base
| MCP:meqhpA | 	$(ci-compose) run --rm unit pre-commit run -a
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | cleantest:  ## Clean up test containers
| MCP:meqhpA | 	$(ci-compose) kill
| MCP:meqhpA | 	$(ci-compose) down --remove-orphans
| MCP:meqhpA | 	$(ci-compose) build
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | ## Migrations
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | migrations: ## Generate a migration using alembic
| MCP:meqhpA | ifeq ($(m),)
| MCP:meqhpA | 	@echo "Specify a message with m={message} and a rev-id with revid={revid} (e.g. 0001 etc.)"; exit 1
| MCP:meqhpA | else ifeq ($(revid),)
| MCP:meqhpA | 	@echo "Specify a message with m={message} and a rev-id with revid={revid} (e.g. 0001 etc.)"; exit 1
| MCP:meqhpA | else
| MCP:meqhpA | 	docker compose run api alembic revision --autogenerate -m "$(m)" --rev-id="$(revid)"
| MCP:meqhpA | endif
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | migrate: ## Run migrations upgrade using alembic
| MCP:meqhpA | 	docker compose run --rm api alembic upgrade head
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | downgrade: ## Run migrations downgrade using alembic
| MCP:meqhpA | 	docker compose run --rm api alembic downgrade -1
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | help: ## Display this help message
| MCP:meqhpA | 	@awk -F '##' '/^[a-z_]+:[a-z ]+##/ { print "\033[34m"$$1"\033[0m" "\n" $$2 }' Makefile

| MCP:meqhpA | version: "3.7"
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | services:
| MCP:meqhpA |   api:
| MCP:meqhpA |     depends_on:
| MCP:meqhpA |       - db
| MCP:meqhpA |     ports:
| MCP:meqhpA |       - "127.0.0.1:5000:5000"
| MCP:meqhpA |     build:
| MCP:meqhpA |       dockerfile: ./api/Dockerfile
| MCP:meqhpA |       context: .
| MCP:meqhpA |     volumes:
| MCP:meqhpA |       - ./api:/home/user/app/api
| MCP:meqhpA |     environment:
| MCP:meqhpA |       - DB_STRING=postgresql://postgres:test@db:5432/postgres
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA |   db:
| MCP:meqhpA |     image: postgres:14.5
| MCP:meqhpA |     ports:
| MCP:meqhpA |       - "127.0.0.1:5432:5432"
| MCP:meqhpA |     volumes:
| MCP:meqhpA |       - postgres-data:/var/lib/postgresql/data
| MCP:meqhpA |     environment:
| MCP:meqhpA |       - POSTGRES_PASSWORD=test
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | volumes:
| MCP:meqhpA |   postgres-data:

| MCP:meqhpA | # Fastapi Project Template
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | A FastAPI project template using Make, Docker Compose and Github Actions,
| MCP:meqhpA | that includes **SQLAlchemy**, **Alembic** and an integration test setup.
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | [![Code Integration](https://github.com/bitestreams/fastapi-template/actions/workflows/code-integration.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/bitestreams/fastapi-template/actions/workflows/code-integration.yml)
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | ##  Installation
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | The only requirements to develop are **Docker** and **Make**.
| MCP:meqhpA | Docker makes it easy to get started and enables easier switching between projects, operating systems and machines.
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | To start up the project locally you first clone the project, and then run the following command in the cloned directory:
| MCP:meqhpA | ```sh
| MCP:meqhpA | $ git clone https://github.com/Donnype/fastapi-template.git
| MCP:meqhpA | $ cd fastapi-template
| MCP:meqhpA | $ make up
| MCP:meqhpA | ```
| MCP:meqhpA | Then to update the schema using alembic, in another shell:
| MCP:meqhpA | ```sh
| MCP:meqhpA | $ make migrate
| MCP:meqhpA | ```
| MCP:meqhpA | **That's it**. The app is set up and should be running at [localhost:5000/docs](localhost:5000/docs).
| MCP:meqhpA | Code changes are automatically detected using a mounted docker volume.
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | ### Makefile
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | ---
| MCP:meqhpA | The Makefile is the 'entrypoint' for the tools in this structure,
| MCP:meqhpA | such that you can easily run different commands without remembering the exact arguments.
| MCP:meqhpA | Run `make help` to get an overview of the available commands:
| MCP:meqhpA | ```sh
| MCP:meqhpA | $ make help
| MCP:meqhpA | up:
| MCP:meqhpA |  Run the application
| MCP:meqhpA | done: check test
| MCP:meqhpA |  Prepare for a commit
| MCP:meqhpA | test: utest itest
| MCP:meqhpA |  Run unit and integration tests
| MCP:meqhpA | utest: cleantest
| MCP:meqhpA |  Run unit tests
| MCP:meqhpA | itest: cleantest
| MCP:meqhpA |  Run integration tests
| MCP:meqhpA | check: cleantest
| MCP:meqhpA |  Check the code base
| MCP:meqhpA | cleantest:
| MCP:meqhpA |  Clean up test containers
| MCP:meqhpA | migrations:
| MCP:meqhpA |  Generate a migration using alembic
| MCP:meqhpA | migrate:
| MCP:meqhpA |  Run migrations upgrade using alembic
| MCP:meqhpA | downgrade:
| MCP:meqhpA |  Run migrations downgrade using alembic
| MCP:meqhpA | help:
| MCP:meqhpA |  Display this help message
| MCP:meqhpA | ```
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | Make allows you to collect common scripts and commands for the project.
| MCP:meqhpA | Other projects using different programming languages and frameworks get the same development interface by using Make.
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | ---
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | ### Installation without Docker
| MCP:meqhpA | To install a local copy of the python environment (to get code-completion for example),
| MCP:meqhpA | you must have **Poetry** installed.
| MCP:meqhpA | Create a poetry environment by running `poetry install` at the root of the project.
| MCP:meqhpA | To start up the server in the poetry environment and talk to a test sqlite database in `./test.db`, run
| MCP:meqhpA | ```bash
| MCP:meqhpA | $ export DB_STRING=sqlite:///test.db/
| MCP:meqhpA | $ poetry run alembic upgrade head && poetry run uvicorn api.main:app --port 5000 --reload
| MCP:meqhpA | ```
| MCP:meqhpA | Again, the app should be all set up and running at [localhost:5000/docs](localhost:5000/docs).
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | ### Tests
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | ---
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | The tests are containerized and the Docker setup can be found in the `.ci/` folder.
| MCP:meqhpA | They are written using Pytest.
| MCP:meqhpA | You can run the tests using:
| MCP:meqhpA | ```bash
| MCP:meqhpA | $ make test
| MCP:meqhpA | ```
| MCP:meqhpA | This runs the integration & unit tests.
| MCP:meqhpA | If you want to run them separately, use `make itest` to run the integration tests and `make utest` to run the unit tests.
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | ## Further reading
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | To read about the benefits of using this template,
| MCP:meqhpA | check out [my blog post](https://bitestreams.com/blog/fastapi_template/).
| MCP:meqhpA | successfully downloaded text file (SHA: c2b235985b32d335f0ccbd5a6fc7d1c40d867c01)

| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | [alembic]
| MCP:meqhpA | script_location = api/migrations
| MCP:meqhpA | prepend_sys_path = .
| MCP:meqhpA | version_path_separator = os  # Use os.pathsep. Default configuration used for new projects.
| MCP:meqhpA | sqlalchemy.url = driver://user:pass@localhost/dbname
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | [post_write_hooks]
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | [loggers]
| MCP:meqhpA | keys = root,sqlalchemy,alembic
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | [handlers]
| MCP:meqhpA | keys = console
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | [formatters]
| MCP:meqhpA | keys = generic
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | [logger_root]
| MCP:meqhpA | level = WARN
| MCP:meqhpA | handlers = console
| MCP:meqhpA | qualname =
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | [logger_sqlalchemy]
| MCP:meqhpA | level = WARN
| MCP:meqhpA | handlers =
| MCP:meqhpA | qualname = sqlalchemy.engine
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | [logger_alembic]
| MCP:meqhpA | level = INFO
| MCP:meqhpA | handlers =
| MCP:meqhpA | qualname = alembic
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | [handler_console]
| MCP:meqhpA | class = StreamHandler
| MCP:meqhpA | args = (sys.stderr,)
| MCP:meqhpA | level = NOTSET
| MCP:meqhpA | formatter = generic
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | [formatter_generic]
| MCP:meqhpA | format = %(levelname)-5.5s [%(name)s] %(message)s
| MCP:meqhpA | datefmt = %H:%M:%S

data.content: Let me also check the API Dockerfile and the migrations setup:

content.text:
| MCP:meqhpA | FROM python:3.10-slim as base
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | # Create a non-root user to run the app with.
| MCP:meqhpA | RUN groupadd --gid 1000 user &&  adduser --disabled-password --gecos '' --uid 1000 --gid 1000 user
| MCP:meqhpA | USER user
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | WORKDIR /home/user/app
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | # Install Poetry.
| MCP:meqhpA | RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache pip install --user poetry==1.4.2
| MCP:meqhpA | ENV \
| MCP:meqhpA |     PATH="/home/user/.local/bin:/home/user/app/.venv/bin:${PATH}" \
| MCP:meqhpA |     PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
| MCP:meqhpA |     POETRY_NO_INTERACTION=1 \
| MCP:meqhpA |     POETRY_VIRTUALENVS_IN_PROJECT=true
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | COPY --chown=user:user ./pyproject.toml ./poetry.lock ./alembic.ini ./
| MCP:meqhpA | RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache poetry install
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | FROM base as dev
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | COPY --chown=user:user ./api api
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | CMD ["uvicorn", "api.main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "5000", "--reload"]
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | FROM base as test
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | USER root
| MCP:meqhpA | RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git-core
| MCP:meqhpA | USER user
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache poetry install --with dev
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | # For running pre-commit
| MCP:meqhpA | COPY --chown=user:user ./.git .git
| MCP:meqhpA | COPY --chown=user:user ./.pre-commit-config.yaml .pre-commit-config.yaml
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | RUN mkdir /home/user/.mypy_cache && chown -R user:user /home/user/.mypy_cache
| MCP:meqhpA | RUN pre-commit install --install-hooks
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | COPY --chown=user:user ./api api
| MCP:meqhpA | 
| MCP:meqhpA | # Default target.
| MCP:meqhpA | FROM dev

data.content:
Here's a summary of how to start this FastAPI project locally with Docker and how to apply database migrations:

---

## Starting the Project Locally with Docker

**Prerequisites:** Docker and Make must be installed.

1. **Clone the repository and enter the directory:**
   ```sh
   git clone <repo-url>
   cd T04_R01
   ```

2. **Start the application:**
   ```sh
   make up
   ```
   This runs `docker compose up -d --build api`, which:
   - Builds the API image from `api/Dockerfile` (Python 3.10-slim with Poetry, uvicorn on port 5000).
   - Starts a **PostgreSQL 14.5** database container (`db` service, password: `test`).
   - The API service connects to the DB via `DB_STRING=postgresql://postgres:test@db:5432/postgres`.
   - The `./api` directory is volume-mounted so code changes are hot-reloaded automatically.

3. **Access the app** at **[localhost:5000/docs](http://localhost:5000/docs)** (Swagger UI).

---

## Applying Database Migrations (Alembic)

The project uses **Alembic** for schema migrations. Migrations live in `api/migrations/` and are configured via `alembic.ini` (with the `DB_STRING` environment variable set by Docker Compose).

| Action | Command | What it does |
|---|---|---|
| **Apply all pending migrations** | `make migrate` | Runs `docker compose run --rm api alembic upgrade head` — brings the database schema up to date. |
| **Generate a new migration** | `make migrations m="<description>" revid="<id>"` | Runs `docker compose run api alembic revision --autogenerate -m "<description>" --rev-id="<id>"` — auto-generates a migration script based on model changes. (Both `m` and `revid` are required, e.g. `revid=0001`.) |
| **Roll back one migration** | `make downgrade` | Runs `docker compose run --rm api alembic downgrade -1` — reverts the last applied migration. |

---

## Without Docker (optional, for local development / IDE autocomplete)

```sh
poetry install
export DB_STRING=sqlite:///test.db/
poetry run alembic upgrade head
poetry run uvicorn api.main:app --port 5000 --reload
```

This uses a local SQLite database instead of PostgreSQL.

---

**In short:** `make up` starts everything via Docker Compose (FastAPI + PostgreSQL), and `make migrate` applies Alembic database migrations — that's all you need to get running.
